quote: The window expires by itself: the expiry is tracked with a `Process.send_after(self(), {:maintenance_end, service_name}, duration_ms)` timer. On expiry, `notify.(service_name, :maintenance_ended, nil)` fires and the service returns to normal monitoring, its reported status reverting to its current health.
reason: The prompt states explicitly that a maintenance window expires on its own via a `Process.send_after` timer of `duration_ms` and that on expiry the reported `:status` reverts to the underlying health (here `:up`), so a service left reporting `:maintenance` after the window elapsed contradicts the spec; the only documented reasons to ignore a `{:maintenance_end, name}` message are an unknown service or one no longer in maintenance, so gating expiry on any additional condition (e.g. comparing a possibly-frozen `:clock` against the deadline) is solver-invented, not prompt-sanctioned.
